title: Present Tense - E to IE Stem Change
description: Learn how certain Spanish verbs change from E to IE in the present tense.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: What was the word for 'what'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And do you remember how to say 'to pass'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Pasar. This can also mean 'to happen'. How do you say 'he passes'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. And 'she passes'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And 'it passes'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'what happens' or 'what's going on'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. If you want to say 'I want to know what's happening', how would you say it?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero saber qué pasa. Now, let's look at verbs like 'tener' and 'querer'. These verbs have an E in their root that changes to IE when stressed. How do you say 'I want'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero. Notice how 'querer' becomes 'quiero'. The E splits into IE. The same happens with 'tener'. How do you say 'he has'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Tiene. Let's try another verb: 'venir'. How do you say 'he comes'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Viene. Good. Now, 'pensar' means 'to think'. How do you say 'I think'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Pienso. And 'he thinks'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Piensa. How would you say 'what do you think'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Qué piensas? If you say 'I think to eat', it means 'I plan on eating'. How would you say that?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Pienso comer. How would you say 'I plan on eating later'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Pienso comer más tarde. Now, 'entender' means 'to understand'. How do you say 'I understand'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Entiendo. And 'he understands'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Entiende. Now, 'perder' means 'to lose'. How do you say 'I lose'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Pierdo. How would you say 'I lose it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Lo pierdo. Good. And 'I lose them'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Los pierdo. Good.

outro.text: You've learned how certain verbs in Spanish change from E to IE in the present tense. This pattern will help you with many verbs.
Try using these verbs in sentences. How would you say 'I want to understand' or 'he plans to come'?
These patterns make it easier to predict how verbs behave. Keep practicing to get more comfortable with them.
